title: MCP tool surface drift — read-only registry vs hidden write capability
stakeholder_intent: The MCP agent must not escalate to write or admin capabilities. No privilege escalation of any kind is permitted. The callable tool surface must match the declared read-only registry.
formalized_policy: MCP tools/list registers read-only GitHub tools only. The seccomp profile uses a syscall denylist blocking ptrace and clone. CAP_SYS_ADMIN is dropped.
implementation_claim: create_pull_request is absent from tools/list, but tools/call still executes write tools. CAP_SYS_ADMIN is unavailable, but setuid binaries are allowed to execute.
expected_issue: Operational analogue of tools/list vs tools/call mismatch: policy claims a bounded read surface, but the implementation claim admits a privilege escalation vector (setuid) not covered by the denylist — implication fails before any live MCP probe.
